what_it_does: A static HTML/CSS composition presents one narrative broken into one-page, three-page, and eight-page versions inside a 1600x900 framed view.
observations: Uses a centered 16:9 artboard with absolute title, row stack, footer wash, blueprint texture, and circular V mark.
Builds three horizontal tonal zones labeled One Page, Three Pages, and Eight Pages.
Represents narrative granularity through page-card counts: one wide card, three medium cards, and eight narrow cards.
Uses CSS custom properties for color, spacing, typography, radius, and shared surface tokens.
Highlights the number 365 with a gold star-colored span across multiple story titles.
standouts: The visual system treats narrative count as spatial structure: one, three, and eight card groups occupy equal-tier rows.
The file explicitly encodes the idea that each tier is a zone, not a separate plate.
The page labels map each card to story roles such as problem, way, proof, route, hands, and close.
conventions: Single-file HTML document with embedded CSS and minimal DOM structure.
Uses semantic class names for layout roles such as rows, trow, pages, pg, title, foot, and mark.
Relies on CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css rather than hard-coded design values.
Uses flex layout for row and card distribution, with absolute positioning for the overall artboard elements.
Includes a file-local design note in a CSS comment explaining the zone treatment.
concerns: The 1600x900 fixed canvas and absolute positioning make the layout viewport-specific.
Rendering depends on external CSS variables and a Google Fonts request that are not defined in this file.
Several page titles are long relative to the narrow eight-page cards, so text fit depends on the chosen token values.
notes: Brief this file as a tiered narrative diagram showing the same story compressed or expanded across one, three, and eight page counts.
questions: What does the circular V mark denote in this composition?
Is the eight-page row intended as a literal page sequence or as a visual index of possible sections?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title and labels match the actual composition: it shows one story told at three counts.
apparent_themes: tonal zones instead of isolated cards
intention_signals: It seems intended to make narrative modularity visible by showing that the same underlying story can be fused, grouped, or expanded while retaining sequence and roles.
novelty: The distinct move is using page-count tiers as full-width tonal zones, with card width and quantity carrying the structural meaning.
contribution: This file contributes a compact visual grammar for expressing one narrative at multiple granularities, keeping the tier labels, role labels, page-card rhythm, and zone-based layout as reusable design material.
summary_for_embedding: Static 1600x900 HTML/CSS artboard visualizing one story as one-page, three-page, and eight-page narrative tiers with labeled page cards.
intention_for_embedding: Created to show how the same workshop-led story can be compressed or expanded while preserving ordered roles and a shared visual system.
